cue: How do you say you don't know what to do?

es.text: No saber a qué carta quedarse
es.literal: not to know which card to stay with
es.meaning: To be unable to decide; to not know which way to go.
es.example: Con tantas ofertas no sé a qué carta quedarme.
es.exampleTranslation: With so many offers I don't know which to pick.

it.literal: not to know which fish to catch
it.meaning: To be confused; to not know what to do.
it.example: Con tante offerte non so che pesce prendere.
it.exampleTranslation: With so many offers I don't know which way to turn.

bridge.note: Indecision staged as two different games. Italian puts you at the water with a net and too many fish; Spanish sits you at a card table, unable to commit to a hand. Fishing is luck, cards are nerve — and the Spanish version quietly admits that choosing badly is a bet you can lose, where the Italian one just leaves you standing in the shallows.

interference.es_to_it: 'Non sapere a che carta restare' means nothing in Italian; the Italian dilemma is aquatic — che pesce prendere.
interference.it_to_es: 'No saber qué pez agarrar' is not Spanish. The fixed refrán deals cards: a qué carta quedarse.

review.notes: Discovered via an Instagram card set Joe shared 2026-07-20 (discovery only; all glosses and examples authored fresh). ES counterpart chosen by Claude from recall — UNVERIFIED. Luck-vs-nerve framing; both are B2 and worth pairing on adjacent weeks.
